etymology.description: 旡(旡) (phonetic) + 心(心) (semantic, 'heart') -> 夊(夊) ('foot') added for depiction of person -> corrupted to 爫冖
etymology.interpretation: The character 愛(愛) (ài, 'love') combines the semantic component 心(心) (xīn, 'heart') with the phonetic component 旡(旡), representing feelings from the heart. The 夊(夊) ('foot') was later added, reflecting a common stylistic practice adding human elements, turning the original form into 𢙴 and evolving further into the current form.
etymology.simplification: The traditional component 爫(爪) ('claw') with 冖(冖) ('cover') was simplified in 爱(愛) by replacing 爫冖 with 友(友) (yǒu, 'friend').

examples.chinese: 我爱我的家人(我愛我的家人)。
爱是一种美好的情感(愛是一種美好的情感)。
examples.pinyin: Ài shì yī zhǒng měihǎo de qínggǎn.

examples.chinese: 她总是用心去爱身边的人(她總是用心去愛身邊的人)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒngshì yòng xīn qù ài shēnbiān de rén.
examples.english: She always loves the people around her wholeheartedly.
